title: The Collector
desc: Rare minerals grow in the deep walls. Getting them out is the problem.

steps.text:
The walls near Gothos Tower are veined with minerals that don't exist anywhere else in Perim — dark crystals with a metallic sheen that the smiths call "shadow-iron." It's stronger than regular iron, lighter, and commands a premium.

The problem is location. Van Bloot's territory. The minerals belong to whoever can take them, but taking them means working in a place where courage drops like temperature and every shadow seems to lean toward you.

A collector has been gathering shadow-iron for weeks. She's fast, efficient, and visibly nervous. "I work the outer walls only. Deeper in, the crystals are bigger but the corridors get... wrong. Angles that don't add up. Rooms that are bigger inside than outside."

options.label: Help collect from the outer walls — safe zone
Go deeper for the bigger crystals

steps.text:
The outer walls are manageable. The shadow-iron grows in clusters — dark, glinting veins that you chisel free in chunks. The work is methodical: find a vein, score around it, lever it loose, bag it.

The crystals are cold. Not ambient-temperature cold — actively cold. They pull heat from your hands. The collector wears thick gloves and works fast.

You fill two bags in an hour. Good yield. The collector counts the pieces and nods. "That's enough for three shield-rims and a sword blank. The smiths will be happy."

As you leave, you glance down the corridor toward the deeper rooms. The angles are fine from here. But you notice the shadows — they're all pointing the same direction. Toward the center of the tower. Even yours.

You walk away faster than necessary.

options.label: Deliver the crystals and don't look back

steps.text:
The deeper corridors are exactly as described. A room that should be ten feet square is somehow twenty. A hallway turns right, right, right, and right again — but you don't end up where you started. The geometry is hostile.

But the crystals are magnificent. Shards as long as your forearm, growing in clusters that ring like bells when you touch them. You fill a bag in minutes.

Then you notice the quiet. Not silence — the outer walls were silent too. This is different. This is the quiet of something listening.

You take your bag and navigate back. The hallways cooperate — mostly. You only take one wrong turn, and the wrong turn leads to the exit anyway, which shouldn't be possible.

The collector looks at your haul and goes pale. "Those are core crystals. Those don't come loose easily." She examines them. "They're pre-cut. Someone — or something — already chiseled them free."

You both look at the tower. You both decide not to go back.

options.label: Take the crystals and leave questions unasked
